description:
Goal
Close the any/none burn-down by removing or proving the remaining semantic top/bottom producers, including closure-call uncertainty, non-arithmetic operator typing, unary negation, and union identities.

Signal
- Remaining `any_ty(world)` calls are explicit dynamic/source top boundaries with tests; remaining `none_ty(world)` calls are true empty/no-return/impossible-input bottoms with tests.
- Closure-call non-callable or ambiguous callable cases do not silently become top unless the source semantics are intentionally dynamic.
- Non-arithmetic operators such as cons/list concat/range have either real function/spec-backed typing or explicitly documented dynamic semantics; arithmetic remains routed through functions/intrinsics, not binop special cases.
- A final grep/deletion check is recorded before `fz-rh2.18.8` starts.

Tactics
- Finish the audit categories from `.7.1` in dependency order instead of chasing local convenience.
- Prefer deleting special cases by routing through ordinary functions/specs where that is the language model.
- Tighten tests to source spelling when distinct spellings matter; otherwise keep one intent test with fixture comments.

Outro
No unclassified `any_ty`/`none_ty` in compiler2 semantic flow, no revived `binop_ty` arithmetic special case, no closure-call top fallback for missing evidence, and no leftover broad top/bottom producer without a blocking ticket.
title: Compiler2 type convergence: close semantic any/none burn-down
